---
title: 'Rush Game'
author: "Our Group"
date: "2026-04-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r rush}
cfb_all <- read.csv("cfb_all.csv")
library(dplyr)
champions <- read.csv("champions.csv")
cfb_rush <- cfb_all %>%
  select(First.Down.Runs, Rushing.Off.Rank, Rush.Attempts, Rush.Yds, Rushing.TD, Rushing.Yards.per.Game, Rushing.Def.Rank, Rush.Yards.Per.Game.Allowed)
cfb_rush
cfb_all
champions
champions_clean <- champions %>%
  mutate(
    Team = str_remove(Champion, "\\s*\\(.*\\)")
  ) %>%
  select(Year, Team)
```

```{r rush_comp}
library(stringr)

cfb_all_net <- cfb_all %>%
  mutate(
    Conference = str_extract(Team, "\\(.*\\)"),
    Conference = str_remove_all(Conference, "[()]"),
    Team = str_remove(Team, "\\s*\\(.*\\)"),
    net.rush.ypg = Rushing.Yards.per.Game - Rush.Yards.Per.Game.Allowed
  )

cfb_all_net
wins_summary <- cfb_all_net %>%
  group_by(Win) %>%
  summarise(
    avg.net.rush.ypg = mean(net.rush.ypg, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_teams = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
wins_conf_summary <- cfb_all_net %>%
  group_by(Conference, Win) %>%
  summarise(
    avg.net.rush.ypg = mean(net.rush.ypg, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_teams = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

wins_conf_summary <- wins_conf_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Conference = case_when(
      Conference %in% c("C-USA", "CUSA") ~ "Conference USA",
      Conference %in% c("MAC", "OH MAC") ~ "MAC",
      Conference %in% c("MWC", "Mountain West") ~ "Mountain West",
      Conference %in% c("Independent", "FBS Independent", "") ~ "Independent",
      Conference %in% c("ACC", "FL ACC", "AAC") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ Conference
    )
  )
wins_conf_summary
```

```{r rush_plot}
ggplot(
  wins_conf_summary,
  aes(x = Win, y = avg.net.rush.ypg, color = Conference)
) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = 0,
           fill = "red", alpha = 0.08) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 0, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "green", alpha = 0.08) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "ACC" = "#D55E00",
      "Big 12" = "#E69F00",
      "Big Ten" = "#009E73",
      "Conference USA" = "#56B4E9",
      "Independent" = "#000000",
      "MAC" = "#0072B2",
      "Mountain West" = "#CC79A7",
      "Pac-12" = "#F0E442",
      "SEC" = "#7F7F7F",
      "Sun Belt" = "#A6761D"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Wins vs Net Rushing Yards per Game",
    subtitle = "Green = positive rushing margin | Red = negative rushing margin",
    x = "Wins",
    y = "Average Net Rushing Yards per Game",
    color = "Conference"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r champs_rush}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
team_main_colors <- c(
  "Alabama"      = "#9E1B32",  # Crimson
  "Clemson"      = "#F56600",  # Orange
  "Georgia"      = "#BA0C2F",  # Red
  "LSU"          = "#461D7C",  # Purple
  "Ohio St."     = "#BB0000",  # Scarlet
  "Florida St."  = "#782F40"   # Garnet
)

team_secondary_colors <- c(
  "Alabama"      = "#828A8F",  # Silver
  "Clemson"      = "#522D80",  # Purple
  "Georgia"      = "#000000",  # Black
  "LSU"          = "#FDD023",  # Gold
  "Ohio St."     = "#FFFFFF",  # White
  "Florida St."  = "#CEB888"   # Gold
)
champions_clean <- champions %>%
  mutate(
    Team = str_remove(Champion, "\\s*\\(.*\\)")
  ) %>%
  select(Year, Team)
champ_keys <- paste(champions_clean$Year, champions_clean$Team)
cfb_champ_compare <- cfb_all_net %>%
  mutate(
    season_key = paste(Year, Team),
    is_champion = season_key %in% champ_keys
  ) %>%
  filter(Team %in% champions_clean$Team)
ggplot(
  cfb_champ_compare,
  aes(x = Year, y = net.rush.ypg, group = Team)
) +
  # Connecting lines (MAIN team color)
  geom_line(
    aes(color = Team),
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +

  # Non-championship points (SECONDARY team color)
  geom_point(
    data = subset(cfb_champ_compare, !is_champion),
    aes(fill = Team),
    color = "black",
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    alpha = 0.85,
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +

  # Championship seasons = GOLD STARS
  geom_point(
    data = subset(cfb_champ_compare, is_champion),
    shape = 8,
    color = "gold",
    size = 4.5,
    stroke = 1.2
  ) +

  # Zero reference line
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "gray50"
  ) +

  # Color scales
  scale_color_manual(values = team_main_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = team_secondary_colors) +

  labs(
    title = "Rushing Performance of National Champions Over Time",
    subtitle = "Lines = team primary color | Points = secondary color | Gold stars = championship seasons",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Net Rushing Yards per Game",
    color = "Team",
    fill = "Team"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
  breaks = seq(
    min(cfb_champ_compare$Year),
    max(cfb_champ_compare$Year),
    by = 1
  )
  ) +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )


```
